steps.title: Identify the comparison structure
steps.content: The passage opens with “Like this alabaster box... is my heart,” using a simile to compare one physical subject, the alabaster box, with one abstract subject, the speaker's heart. The following lines extend that comparison.

steps.title: Apply to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A (‘They describe the subjects, one physical and one abstract, of a comparison that extends throughout the text.’) identifies exactly this function—the two underlined portions are the subjects (one physical, one abstract) of a comparison that extends throughout the text.

The other three choices misread the passage by imposing contrasts, decisions, or changes that the text does not contain.

That confirms A is correct.
